 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.22","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] CI closure proof: capture successful CI run on main","description":"## Gap\nwa-nu4.3.9 requires cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, and E2E artifact behavior to pass in CI. As of audit HEAD 8759ac8c, gh run list --workflow=CI --limit 5 showed the latest CI run queued (databaseId 25021655622, head 051f3b67) and recent prior CI runs cancelled, so the audit could verify workflow wiring but not a green current run.\n\n## Deliverable\n1. Inspect the latest main CI run with gh run list --workflow=CI --limit 5 --json databaseId,status,conclusion,headSha,createdAt,displayTitle.\n2. If a current run is green, append the run id/headSha/conclusion evidence to wa-nu4.3.9 and close this bead.\n3. If the run fails, file or fix the smallest concrete blocker bead and leave wa-nu4.3.9 open.\n4. Confirm E2E/artifact upload jobs are present in the run summary, not only in YAML.\n\n## Acceptance\n- A successful CI run on main is cited by databaseId, headSha, and conclusion=success.\n- Any failure is decomposed into specific follow-up beads rather than blocking the epic anonymously.\n- Scope stays to evidence collection plus one tiny blocker fix if obvious.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.417033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:28:29.296733Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:28:29.296572Z","close_reason":"Evidence collected: 100/100 last main runs not green (97 cancelled, 1 failure 24973856126 at e549b5e2, 2 in-progress→cancelled, 0 success). Workflow_dispatch attempt 25033837579 (headSha 11e22da0) cancelled within 30s by next push. Structural blocker filed as wa-nu4.3.9.29 (concurrency cancel-in-progress + heavy swarm push rate). Evidence comment added to wa-nu4.3.9; epic remains OPEN per bead spec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.22","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:22.417033Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.23","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] CI: get latest ci.yml quality gate green and attach failure artifacts","description":"## Gap
The ship-readiness epic requires cargo fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test, and E2E to pass in CI. The workflow wiring exists, but live GitHub evidence is not green: gh run view 25021655622 shows the latest ci.yml run for HEAD 051f3b67 is still status=queued overall, with Lint (macos-14) failed at Check formatting and Test (macos-14) failed at Run tests; many other jobs remain queued. gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --status=success --limit 3 returned [].

## Scope
1. Inspect the completed logs/artifacts for run 25021655622 once GitHub exposes them.
2. Fix the concrete fmt/test failures if they are repo issues and fit in one hour; otherwise file exact child bugs with log excerpts.
3. Re-run ci.yml on main and capture a successful run URL, or document the remaining runner/infra blocker.

## Acceptance
- A ci.yml run at or after 051f3b67 completes successfully, or every non-green job has a linked concrete bug bead with file/log evidence.
- The bead close reason includes gh run URLs and job names.
- E2E artifact upload is confirmed from the completed run artifacts when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:53.001607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:25:16.425336Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:25:16.425085Z","close_reason":"Fixed concrete ci.yml failures from run 25021655622 (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/actions/runs/25021655622): Lint (macos-14) job 73283310901 failed at Check formatting and Test (macos-14) job 73283310853 failed at Run tests. Fix commits on main: 913ce5cb adds serde_json for frankenterm-core-telemetry-types tests, 7f466611 rustfmt sweep, b5188a58 adds target/ci-logs teeing so lint/test failures upload artifacts. Artifact download for old lint-logs-macos-14/test-logs-macos-14 returned no valid artifacts because old steps produced no files; b5188a58 fixes that for future failures. Verified via rch cargo fmt --all -- --check, rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core-telemetry-types --lib (211 passed), and rch cargo check -p frankenterm-core-telemetry-types --all-targets. Remote rerun b5188a58: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/actions/runs/25033710902 was cancelled by newer swarm pushes with zero failures; latest observed ci.yml run 25033814684 for 11e22da0 was queued, so remaining non-green state is GitHub Actions concurrency/runner churn, not the original fmt/test failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","ship-readiness","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.23","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:03:53.001607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.24","title":"CI: record a non-cancelled successful full CI run for ship-readiness","description":"# Purpose
Prove wa-nu4.3.9 CI acceptance with a completed successful `CI` workflow run on `main`, not just static YAML inspection.

# Evidence from ft-v5lz3.4 audit
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` statically defines fmt/clippy/test/e2e/artifact gates.
- `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 5 --json ...` on 2026-04-28 showed latest run queued (`25021655622`) and recent older runs cancelled.
- `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --limit 50 ... | jq '[.[] | select(.conclusion=="success")]'` returned `[]`.

# Scope
1. Wait for or trigger one current `CI` workflow run on `main`.
2. If it succeeds, update this bead with the run URL, head SHA, and a JSON predicate showing `conclusion == "success"`.
3. If it fails or stays queued/cancelled, file or fix the concrete blocker rather than marking wa-nu4.3.9 complete.

# Acceptance
- A non-cancelled `CI` run on `main` has `status=completed` and `conclusion=success`.
- The close reason includes the `gh run view <id> --json status,conclusion,url,headSha` evidence.
- Any failure is decomposed into a concrete bug/task bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:02.059049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:06:27.222656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:06:27.222343Z","close_reason":"Superseded by existing CI proof/failure children wa-nu4.3.9.22 and wa-nu4.3.9.23 from the same ft-v5lz3.4 audit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.24","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:02.059049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Release proof: verify tag workflow assets, metadata, checksums, provenance, reproducibility","description":"## Gap
The release workflow is wired, but there is no live release proof to close wa-nu4.3.9: gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 5 returned [] and gh release view returned 'release not found'. release.yml builds target assets, sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0, runs ft --version / ft version --full / ft --help / ft doctor --json smoke tests, publishes SHA256SUMS, and generates attestations, but no tag/workflow run artifacts were available to verify.

## Scope
1. Run the release workflow on the v0.1.0 candidate tag or an approved release-candidate tag.
2. Verify downloadable assets exist for the release matrix and have matching SHA256SUMS.
3. Run at least one downloaded Unix binary and one macOS binary with ft --version and ft version --full; record build metadata output.
4. Prove reproducibility for one target by building the same commit twice with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 and comparing hashes, or file a narrower bug if that proof fails.
5. Confirm provenance/attestation artifacts exist for archives and checksums.

## Acceptance
- Close reason includes the release run URL, release URL, checked asset names, version output snippets, and hash/provenance evidence.
- If no release should be cut yet, leave the bead open with the explicit blocking condition rather than closing the parent epic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","reproducibility","ship-readiness","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Release proof: verify tag workflow assets, metadata, checksums, provenance, reproducibility","description":"## Gap
The release workflow is wired, but there is no live release proof to close wa-nu4.3.9: gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 5 returned [] and gh release view returned 'release not found'. release.yml builds target assets, sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0, runs ft --version / ft version --full / ft --help / ft doctor --json smoke tests, publishes SHA256SUMS, and generates attestations, but no tag/workflow run artifacts were available to verify.

## Scope
1. Run the release workflow on the v0.1.0 candidate tag or an approved release-candidate tag.
2. Verify downloadable assets exist for the release matrix and have matching SHA256SUMS.
3. Run at least one downloaded Unix binary and one macOS binary with ft --version and ft version --full; record build metadata output.
4. Prove reproducibility for one target by building the same commit twice with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 and comparing hashes, or file a narrower bug if that proof fails.
5. Confirm provenance/attestation artifacts exist for archives and checksums.

## Acceptance
- Close reason includes the release run URL, release URL, checked asset names, version output snippets, and hash/provenance evidence.
- If no release should be cut yet, leave the bead open with the explicit blocking condition rather than closing the parent epic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:41:28.569387Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:41:28.569112Z","close_reason":"Surfaced-blocker duplicate of wa-nu4.3.9.21. Rechecked live state on 2026-04-28: gh workflow list shows .github/workflows/release.yml active (Release id 225005615), but gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 20 returned [], gh release list --limit 20 returned [], gh release view v0.1.0 returned 'release not found', git tag --list 'v*' returned no local tags, and git ls-remote --tags origin 'refs/tags/v*' returned no remote tags. Therefore there are still no tag workflow artifacts, release assets, SHA256SUMS, provenance attestations, or downloaded binaries to verify. This is the same no-tag/no-release/no-artifacts condition recorded on wa-nu4.3.9.21; parent epic must remain open until an approved release candidate tag/run exists.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","reproducibility","ship-readiness","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.25","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:03.801007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.26","title":"Release: run release workflow and verify published artifacts/provenance","description":"# Purpose
Prove the release half of wa-nu4.3.9 with an actual release workflow run and published artifacts. Static `release.yml` coverage is not enough to close the ship-readiness epic.

# Evidence from ft-v5lz3.4 audit
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` builds linux amd64/arm64, darwin amd64/arm64, and windows amd64; smokes `ft --version`, `ft version --full`, `ft --help`, and `ft doctor --json`; creates checksums; and uses `actions/attest`.
- `gh run list --workflow=release.yml --limit 20 --json ...` returned `[]`.
- `gh release view --json ...` returned `release not found`; `gh release list --limit 10` returned no releases.

# Scope
1. Once the release candidate tag/ref is approved, run or observe `.github/workflows/release.yml`.
2. Verify every matrix build completed and uploaded the expected archive/checksum assets.
3. Verify the GitHub release exists, assets are downloadable, checksums match, and provenance attestations were generated.
4. Record smoke-test evidence for `ft --version`, `ft version --full`, `ft --help`, and `ft doctor --json`.

# Acceptance
- Close reason includes release workflow run URL, tag, asset list, checksum/provenance evidence, and binary smoke output summary.
- If the workflow cannot be run because no release candidate tag exists, leave this bead open with that precise blocker.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:12.947116Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:08:27.348030Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:07:27.322837Z","close_reason":"Superseded by existing release proof children wa-nu4.3.9.21 and wa-nu4.3.9.25 from the same ft-v5lz3.4 audit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.26","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:12.947116Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.27","title":"CI: close macOS/Linux E2E coverage gap from ship-readiness epic","description":"# Purpose
Resolve the remaining E2E coverage ambiguity in wa-nu4.3.9. The epic explicitly calls for E2E tests on Linux and macOS where Docker is required; current CI has several E2E/artifact lanes, but the macOS/Docker portion is not clearly satisfied.

# Evidence from ft-v5lz3.4 audit
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` has `e2e-extended` on `ubuntu-latest` with artifact upload (`e2e-artifacts/`, `e2e-approval-output.log`).
- `.github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml` runs an install E2E on `ubuntu-latest` and `macos-14` and uploads `e2e-artifacts/**`.
- No audited workflow row clearly runs the Docker-required E2E lane on both Linux and macOS, or documents an explicit macOS Docker waiver.

# Scope
1. Decide whether the epic requirement is met by existing non-Docker E2E lanes, or whether a macOS Docker E2E lane/waiver is required.
2. If code/YAML is needed, add the smallest CI change with artifact upload on failure.
3. If macOS Docker is intentionally unsupported in GitHub-hosted CI, document that waiver in the bead and relevant docs/CI comments.

# Acceptance
- `wa-nu4.3.9` has explicit evidence for Linux + macOS E2E coverage, or a documented waiver for macOS Docker.
- The close reason cites exact workflow lines and a `gh run list`/`gh run view` success predicate.
- Any new or changed lane uploads failure artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:24.322262Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:07:27.314391Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:07:27.314152Z","close_reason":"Superseded by existing macOS E2E disposition child wa-nu4.3.9.28 from the same ft-v5lz3.4 audit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.27","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:24.322262Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.28","title":"[wa-nu4.3.9 child] Decide and verify macOS E2E coverage for Docker-dependent ship gate","description":"## Gap
wa-nu4.3.9 explicitly calls for E2E tests on Linux and macOS where Docker is required. The audit found Linux E2E artifact wiring in ci.yml, plus cargo-install validation on ubuntu and macos, but no macOS Docker-style E2E lane equivalent to e2e-extended.

## Scope
1. Decide whether the Docker-dependent E2E gate applies to the current ft E2E harnesses after the project rename.
2. If yes, add or verify a macOS E2E job with artifact upload on failure.
3. If no, update wa-nu4.3.9 notes with the reason and point to the replacement macOS validation lane.

## Acceptance
- The epic has explicit evidence for Linux and macOS E2E expectations, either implemented or deliberately scoped out.
- Any new CI YAML uses actions/upload-artifact on failure or always for relevant logs.
- The close reason cites the workflow file lines and a GitHub Actions run URL when available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_3","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:26.834927Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:33:50.108965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:33:50.108717Z","close_reason":"Decided Linux-only real Docker E2E plus documented hosted-macOS waiver; ci.yml docker-e2e matrix, docs policy, and checklist link shipped in c4894a79. Local YAML/bash/mock-E2E/diff-check/rch cargo check passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","e2e","ship-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.28","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:04:26.834927Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
